name: Water Allocation Lab
description: Model agricultural water delivery as a flow network. Phase 1 finds max-flow/min-cut; Phase 2 selects farms (B&B); Phase 3 schedules irrigations. Stage-wise optimal — not a replacement for the overnight batch solver.

details.summary: Explore real-time agricultural water allocation through computational-theory: max-flow → economic selection → NP-hard scheduling.
learningGoals: See how a pipe network maps to a max-flow graph problem.
Understand which pipes are true bottlenecks via min-cut duality.
Observe how upgrading one pipe shifts the cut to a new bottleneck.
notes: Stage-wise optimal ≠ globally optimal — the pipeline is a deliberate decomposition.
The overnight batch solver remains authoritative for commitment-grade scheduling.
Use this tool to explore 'what if a pipe is upgraded / a farm drops out' scenarios.

complexity.time: Phase 1: O(VE²) Edmonds-Karp. Phase 2: exponential worst-case B&B with heuristic fallback. Phase 3: NP-Hard scheduling.
complexity.space: O(V+E) for the flow network; O(2^n) worst case for B&B

inputs.label: Network Nodes (JSON array)
inputs.description: Array of {id, kind} objects. kind: aquifer | junction | farm.

inputs.label: Pipes (JSON array)
inputs.description: Array of {from, to, capacity} pipe objects.

inputs.label: Farms (JSON array)
inputs.description: Array of {id, nodeId, demand, value, durationHrs, neighbors} farm objects.

inputs.label: Virtual Sink ID
inputs.description: Id for the synthesised super-sink node (created by the adapter — do not add to nodes).
